summary:
Overall UK risk remains Elevated. Cost-of-living and macro sensitivity persists: CPI inflation is 3.3% (12 months to March 2026), rising from 3.0% in February, with motor fuels a key driver; this keeps the outlook exposed to further commodity moves. Brent crude is holding in the mid-$100s per barrel, reinforcing near-term sensitivity in transport and traded-goods costs.

Security and resilience pressures also remain Elevated. Russia’s continued drone strikes on Odesa sustain escalation and Black Sea logistics risk. Domestically, UK cyber risk remains elevated due to urgent patching requirements for widely deployed NetScaler perimeter appliances and warnings that hostile-state-linked cyber activity is intensifying. Near-term UK natural-hazard risk is relatively contained with no active Met Office weather warnings and only limited flood alerts in England.

categories.name: Economic / Financial
categories.rationale: Economic risk remains Elevated: ONS data show CPI inflation at 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026 (up from 3.0% in February), with motor fuels a key upward driver; oil prices remain a near-term sensitivity for transport costs and inflation expectations.
drivers: ONS: CPI inflation 3.3% (12 months to March 2026), up from 3.0% in February; motor fuels were a key upward driver. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/march2026
Trading Economics: Brent crude oil at ~105.33 USD/bbl (Apr/24; page notes last updated April 25, 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

Ofgem: energy price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year (typical dual-fuel household paying by Direct Debit). https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained
OBR (March 2026 EFO): real GDP growth forecast 1.1% in 2026 (0.3pp lower than in November). https://obr.uk/efo/economic-and-fiscal-outlook-march-2026/

categories.name: Military / Security
categories.rationale: European security remains Elevated due to ongoing Russia–Ukraine conflict activity and associated escalation/spillover risk; UK domestic terrorism threat level remains SUBSTANTIAL.
drivers: Reuters: Russian drone attack overnight on Odesa killed two and injured more than a dozen (24 Apr 2026). https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/russian-attack-ukraines-odesa-kills-married-couple-officials-say-2026-04-24/
MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (an attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

categories.name: Political / Governance
categories.rationale: No acute UK governance instability signal identified in reviewed official sources; politics and institutions appear to be functioning normally.
drivers: Institute for Government explainer: COBR is convened for major emergencies/disruption; no new activation signal identified in today’s reviewed sources. https://www.instituteforgovernment.org.uk/explainer/cobr-cobra

categories.name: Natural Hazards
categories.rationale: Near-term natural-hazard risk is Guarded: Met Office shows no active UK weather warnings, and England flood risk remains limited to a small number of alerts.
drivers: Met Office: no active UK weather warnings listed (viewed 25 Apr 2026). https://weather.metoffice.gov.uk/warnings-and-advice/uk-warnings
Environment Agency (GOV.UK): flood alerts and warnings page shows 2 flood alerts (viewed 25 Apr 2026; page includes item update times). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk/alerts-and-warnings

categories.name: Social Stability
categories.rationale: Social disruption risk remains Elevated due to ongoing London Underground industrial action and the prospect of further scheduled strikes extending into May and June.
drivers: BBC: 24-hour Tube strike (midday Thu to midday Fri) with significant line closures/reductions; further strike dates scheduled in May and June. https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cj94x99yex3o

categories.name: Critical Infrastructure / Cyber
categories.rationale: Cyber/CNI risk remains Elevated due to urgent patching requirements for widely deployed NetScaler appliances and ongoing warnings that hostile-state-linked activity is contributing to the most serious UK cyber incidents.
drivers: NCSC: urges immediate action to mitigate Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway vulnerabilities CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368. https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/news/vulnerabilities-affecting-citrix-netscaler-adc-gateway

Reuters (via Daily Maverick): NCSC chief said the NCSC handles about four nationally significant cyber incidents per week on average and that the most serious incidents increasingly originate directly or indirectly from nation states (22 Apr 2026). https://www.dailymaverick.co.za/article/2026-04-22-uk-must-brace-for-rise-in-state-backed-cyberattacks-security-chief-says/

indicators.name: Brent crude
indicators.note: Trading Economics shows Brent crude oil at ~105.33 USD/bbl (Apr/24; page notes last updated April 25, 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

indicators.name: UK CPI inflation (12 months to Mar 2026)
indicators.note: ONS: CPI rose by 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026, up from 3.0% in February; motor fuels made the largest upward contribution to the monthly change. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/march2026

indicators.name: Ofgem energy price cap (typical dual-fuel, Direct Debit)
indicators.note: Ofgem: price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year, down from £1,758 in 1 Jan–31 Mar 2026. https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained

indicators.name: UK terrorism threat level
indicators.note: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

indicators.name: England flood alerts (headline count)
indicators.note: Environment Agency flood alerts and warnings page shows 2 flood alerts (viewed 25 Apr 2026). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk/alerts-and-warnings

changes_since_last_update: Timestamp advanced to 25 April 2026 05:00 UK; overall status held at Elevated.
Brent crude indicator refreshed to ~105.33 USD/bbl (Trading Economics; page notes last updated April 25, 2026).
Natural Hazards view updated: Met Office still shows no active UK weather warnings; flood risk remains limited to a small number of alerts.
Social Stability rationale refreshed with BBC reporting on the latest Tube strike disruption and scheduled future strike dates.
priority_watch_items: Energy/oil sensitivity: monitor whether further Brent moves feed through into UK pump prices and near-term inflation expectations.
London transport disruption: monitor impacts as industrial action continues and as further strike dates in May/June approach.
Perimeter vulnerability exposure: confirm remediation/upgrade progress for NetScaler ADC/Gateway and monitor for exploitation indicators.
European security: monitor for further Russia–Ukraine escalation affecting Black Sea-linked logistics and energy systems.
Flooding: monitor Environment Agency updates for any rapid escalation in local warnings/alerts following rainfall.
source_notes: This update prioritises UK official sources (ONS, Ofgem, MI5, NCSC, Met Office, Environment Agency) with Reuters used for conflict and cyber context; Trading Economics is used for a Brent price snapshot. https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

The Environment Agency site is cookie-bannered and some automated extraction did not consistently capture headline counts; today’s flood-alert count was verified from a page view on 25 Apr 2026. https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk/alerts-and-warnings
